As per the guidelines of the Ministry of Education for PMRF scholars, a Research grant of Rs.2 lakh per year is provided to a PMRF scholar. Thus a scholar is entitled to get RS.10 lakh during the period of the fellowship, subject to PMRF guidelines. The competent authority has approved the guidelines for utilization of PMRF Grant/Contingency, attached as Annexure-A. workshops, seminars, training, short-term courses, fieldwork and other programs aiding the research work of the student. b) Support for International Conferences outside India available only after completion of Comprehensive Examination. A PMRF candidate shal not be eligible for Institute support for conferences within India abroad. c)Entitlement oft ravel and accommodation charges would be as per prevailing rules for the PhD students of the IT Jammu. d) Purchase of one PC/ laptop permitted once during the entire fellowship period. e) Purchase of one tablet/e-reader permitted once during the entire fellowship period. f)Purchase of one printer/scanner or all in one, webcam, keyboard and stylus pen permitted once during8 the entire fellowship period. g) Purchase of hard disk, pen drive, CD and mouse as and when required. h) Payment of publication charges in reputed SCI indexed journals including color page charges, extra page charges, payment of PhD thesis submission fee. i) Life members

international contference outside India may be given subject to a maximum of 80% of the costs involved or the bakanae amount in the grant, whichever is lower. s)Scholars desiring to retain items under (d), (e) and (f) acquired from this grant, may do so at the end or the programme by paying 5% of the acquisition cost to the Institute account (provided it is more than 3 years old). For items less than three years old, the institute depreciation rules would be applicable. EA M JAMMU ECT

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

- UG Programme

- Ph.D Programme
- PMRF

The Prime Minister's Research Fellows (PMRF) Scheme has been designed for improving the quality of research in various higher educational institutions in the country. With attractive fellowships, the scheme seeks to attract the best talent into research thereby realizing the vision of development through innovation. The scheme was announced in the Budget 2018-19. The institutes which can offer PMRF include all the IITs, all the IISERs, Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru and some of the top Central Universities/NITs that offer science and/or technology degrees. The candidates will be selected through a rigorous selection process and their performance will be reviewed suitably through a national convention.

**National Coordinating Institute :**IIT Madras

### About PMRF

The Prime Minister's Research Fellows (PMRF) Scheme has been designed for improving the quality of research in various higher educational institutions in the country. With attractive fellowships, the scheme seeks to attract the best talent into research thereby realizing the vision of development through innovation. The scheme was announced in the Budget 2018-19. The institutes which can offer PMRF include all the IITs, all the IISERs, Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru and some of the top Central Universities/NITs that offer science and/or technology degrees. The candidates will be selected through a rigorous selection process and their performance will be reviewed suitably through a national convention.

**National Coordinating Institute :**IIT Madras

**SAURABH KUMAR JHA**

E-mail: 2022rmt0023@iitjammu.ac.in

Department: Materials Engineering

Institute: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

I am a dedicated PhD candidate in Materials Engineering, specializing in pioneering research on polymers materials for extreme cold climates.

With expertise in synthesis and characterization of polymers, I am excited to contribute in the field of Polymer fiber/textiles for strategic application.

**Research description**

My research domain relates to the field of lightweight advanced textiles for extreme cold climate derived from polymer blend which are characterized by distinct properties of both natural and synthetic fibers. The fact that conventional textiles performance deteriorates in subzero cold climate, compelled us to research and develop a novel textile material, which can be comfortably used by Indian armed forces.
